name: PyInstaller Extractor
description: Carve the Python out of a PyInstaller executable: locate the appended CArchive, extract every entry, and unpack the inner PYZ into individual .pyc files with reconstructed headers — ready for pyc-decompile. Pure stdlib. Static: parses archive structures and marshal.loads the PYZ TOC (deserialization, not execution); never runs the executable or the extracted code.

prerequisites.install_hint: Install Python 3.8+ — pure stdlib, no other dependency.

safety.notes: Static. Parses the CArchive/PYZ byte structures and marshal.loads the PYZ table of contents (deserialization — does NOT execute code objects). Never launches the executable or imports/runs any extracted module. Path-traversal + per-file guards on extraction.

args.name: input
args.desc: a PyInstaller-built executable (onefile or onedir main)

payload.note: Zero-dependency (pyinstxtractor-style). Closes the Python-malware chain: unpack → pyinstaller-extract → pyc-decompile → py-covert-scan. Reconstructs .pyc headers from the PYZ's embedded magic so decompyle3/pyc-decompile can read them (best on 3.7-3.8 targets).
